---
title: "Экология гидроидного полипа Halitholus cirratus"
author: "Я. А. Манылова"
output: 
      word_document:
                reference_docx: Letopis_template.docx
                fig_width: 5
                fig_height: 5

---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(reshape2)
library(knitr)
library(pander)
library(ggplot2)
library(cowplot)
library(readxl)
library(flextable)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyverse)
```

```{r}
meas <- read_xlsx(file.choose(), sheet = "live")
meas$cond1 <- NA
meas$cond1[meas$cond == "l"] <- 0
port <- meas%>%filter(type == "port", cond1 == "0") %>% arrange(length)
port <- port[, c("label", "length", "hydr", "nP")]
```

```{r}
#установка параметров грид в таблице
library(officer)
std_border = fp_border(color="gray", width = 1)
colname <- c("номер пробы", "длина", "наличие гидроризы", "количество полипов")

port %>% 
  flextable() %>% 
  fontsize(part = "all") %>% 
   border_inner_h(border = std_border ) %>% 
  border_inner_v(border = std_border ) %>% 
  fontsize(size = 8, part = "all") %>% 
  set_header_labels(values = colname) %>% 
  colformat_double(j = 1,  big.mark = "", digits = 0) %>% 
  set_caption("Таблица +.1  Размерная структура Portlandia arctica, частота встречаемости и количество полипов Halitholus cirratus")
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
